why_it_generalizes: Composite network design, temporal availability, and modular path residues force multi-mechanism optimization.
seed_example: Choose a connected emergency radio relay subgraph. Links have construction cost, activation windows, and time-varying residues; station-time pairs are penalized if unreachable or only reachable with forbidden storm residues. Minimize cost + outage/resonance penalties. Trap: first N-1 cheapest fallback links are connected but resonate; planted modules and needle stations reward extra timed links.
theme: emergency radio relays with time-windowed interference and modular resonance
brief: [A|BESPOKE-NOVELTY] Author a NOVEL, DEEP, deterministically-scored testlib problem in family 'temporal-network-modular-resilience', objective=minimize, skin 'emergency radio relays with time-windowed interference and modular resonance'. Core idea: choose a connected relay subgraph under link costs, activation windows, and modular path-residue interference; minimize construction cost plus outage/resonance penalties. The cheapest fallback tree is a trap, and the generator includes planted modules, traps, needles, and large envelope-filling cases.

why_it_generalizes: composite facility placement, geometric reach, explicit routed flow, and convex congestion force balanced construction rather than plain set cover
seed_example: Open flood-barrier pumps on a canal grid; every basin must be assigned to an opened pump within Manhattan reach and routed along canal cells. Minimize opening cost + routed distance + squared geometric distance + convex edge-load congestion. Trap: cheap high-radius central pumps cover everything but overload narrow canals; strong solutions spread district pumps and reroute through wider relief canals.
theme: flood-barrier pump placement on a canal grid
brief: [A|BESPOKE-NOVELTY] Author a NOVEL, DEEP, deterministically-scored testlib problem in family 'geometric-flow-congestion-coverage', objective=minimize, skin 'flood-barrier pump placement on a canal grid'. Core idea: Open pumps, assign every basin to an opened pump within Manhattan reach, and output explicit canal routes. Score combines fixed opening cost, geometric distance, route length, and convex congestion on low-width canal edges. The obvious cover-every-basin greedily strategy overloads narrow canals; stronger solutions spread pumps and reroute flow. Include planted/trap/needle generator cases and a real solution ladder.

why_it_generalizes: Cost-sensitive threshold policies under calibration drift, distribution shift, asymmetric misses, and alert-budget constraints generalize across deployed ML decision systems where deterministic policies must trade sensitivity against operational load.
seed_example: Design a deterministic unit-by-acuity alert threshold table for hospital early-warning scores; score on seeded hidden patient streams with distribution shift, asymmetric false-negative cost, alert fatigue, and calibration-bin penalties.
brief: [B] Author a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem: Design a deterministic threshold policy/table for hospital early-warning triage. The evaluator scores seeded synthetic patient streams with distribution shift, asymmetric false-negative cost, alert fatigue penalty, and calibration-bin penalties. Candidate runs isolated stdin->stdout and returns finite thresholds. Objective=maximize utility with a clean bounded Ratio convention.

why_it_generalizes: Constant-weight cover-free codes combine packing, overlap control, and coalition-resistant set systems; local repair/search transfers to combinatorial design and privacy-preserving activation problems.
seed_example: Construct the largest feasible family of binary sensor activation codewords with fixed weight, pairwise overlap cap, and 2-cover-free constraint; score = number of feasible rows versus a deterministic baseline.
brief: [C] Author a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem: Construct the largest constant-weight binary sensor activation code / hypergraph under pairwise overlap caps and 2-cover-free coalition constraints. Theme/domain: privacy-preserving sensor activation codes. Objective=maximize. Follow the format-C contract with deterministic scoring, strict feasibility validation, non-finite rejection, and headroom.

why_it_generalizes: Symbolic scientific-law recovery from noisy partial-regime data tests whether solutions can infer interpretable mechanisms and extrapolate outside the observed domain, not just interpolate samples.
seed_example: Recover a lithium-ion battery degradation law from early-cycle, low-temperature noisy cycling rows; score held-out high-cycle/high-temperature extrapolation error plus expression complexity.
brief: [E] Author a NOVEL, deterministically-scored symbolic-regression problem: infer an interpretable electrochemical battery aging law from noisy early-cycle low-temperature lab data, then minimize held-out high-cycle/high-temperature extrapolation error plus expression complexity. Include multiple regimes/interactions so naive polynomial fitting is a trap; deterministic CPU checker only.

why_it_generalizes: Weighted arithmetic-circuit minimization for sparse polynomial evaluation rewards exact compiler-style CSE, shared monomial reuse, and partial factorization under a strict symbolic equivalence gate.
seed_example: Emit a straight-line arithmetic program or factorization plan for expanded sparse target polynomials, minimizing 3*MUL + ADD/SUB while exactly matching every target; baseline rebuilds every monomial independently.
theme: symbolic compiler for sparse polynomial evaluation
brief: [D|flops] Author a NOVEL, deterministically-scored sparse polynomial compiler problem. Candidate outputs a straight-line arithmetic program/factorization plan; checker verifies exact multivariate polynomial equivalence, rejects malformed/non-finite/oversized outputs, then scores weighted multiplication/addition count against a naive expanded-monomial baseline. Naive Horner/full-monomial CSE should be decent, but stronger solutions exploit shared sparse monomial cores and partial factorization.
